title: chloranthaceae
intro: The taxonomical rank of Chloranthaceae is a family of flowering plants, specifically a group of flowering plants that are characterized by having small, greenish flowers and often solitary stamens. Members of the Chloranthaceae family are typically shrubs or small trees that are native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world. They are often found in shaded areas, such as forests, and are known for their unique foliage and berries. The Chloranthaceae family includes around 62 species, which are divided among 16 genera.
